title: Baiuntu

{{Quote|Any merchant can be bought, my large, furless young friend. That is the nature of business, is it not? To the Qulun, loyalty is just another commodity.|Baiuntu, to Jedi Padawan Anakin Skywalker and his companions|<ref name="The Approaching Storm">''[[The Approaching Storm]]''</ref>}}
'''Baiuntu''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ansionian]] and a non-traditional [[Alwari]] tribesman who was the chief trader of a [[Qulun]] clan on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ansion/Legends|Ansion]].<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] he had [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], their [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], and their [[Ansionian]] guides [[Kyakhta]] and [[Bulgan]] drugged and held captive. He was later [[Death/Legends|killed]] by a [[shanh]] pack during a [[Skirmish in the Qulun camp|skirmish]] in the [[Qulun camp]].<ref name="The Approaching Storm" />

==Biography==
{{Quote|I am Baiuntu, chief trader of this clan faction.|Baiuntu, introducing himself to the Jedi and their companions|<ref name="The Approaching Storm" />}}
'''Baiuntu''' was an obese male Ansonian of the [[Alwari]] tribes who acted as chief trader of a [[Qulun]] clan on [[Ansion/Legends|Ansion]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], along with their [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], and [[Kyakhta|several]] [[Bulgan|local]] [[Tooqui|companions]] encountered a Qulun patrol led by Baiuntu while they were [[Border dispute on Ansion/Legends|searching]] for the [[Borokii]], the ruling [[Alwari]] [[overclan]]. As the Qulun were always keen on company, Baiuntu invited the group to return to [[Qulun camp|their camp]] to dine with them, an offer the Jedi accepted.

After being shown around the camp, the Jedi and their companions were brought into a large portable "trade room" structure, where they were invited to sample various goods offered by the Qulun. After they'd done so, and had been entertained by [[lightwick sculpture]]s and [[dance pixie]]s, the Jedi and Baiuntu discussed [[Politics/Legends|politics]]: Baiuntu felt that the Qulun had benefited from Ansion's membership in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He later invited the group to sample various intoxicating essences, including [[Paluruvu]], which contained a powerful sedative that rendered unconscious the Jedi and their companions—along with Baiuntu, who had willingly inhaled the essence. Baiuntu later admitted the drugging was carried out because of the [[bossban]] [[Soergg Vosadii Bezhin|Soergg]]-issued promise of a large reward to those who could delay the group's return to [[Cuipernam]]. However, the Jedi and their companions were freed by [[Tooqui]] (by way of a [[lorqual]] stampede) prior to a [[Skirmish in the Qulun camp|skirmish]] in the Qulun camp.

Baiuntu, while attempting to pursue the Jedi, was killed by a rabbidly agitated [[shanh]] pack shortly thereafter.

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Merchants are a species unto themselves, it seems. Shape has nothing to do with it. The Qulun realized this from the first time a vessel from another world set down here to trade.|Baiuntu, to the Jedi and their companions|<ref name="The Approaching Storm" />}}
Unlike many Ansionians, Baiuntu chose to shave his mane. Although he was overweight, he was still able to ride a sadain, though the creature had some difficulty carrying him. Baiuntu also considered himself more sophisticated than most among the Qulun clan.

==Behind the scenes==
Baiuntu first appeared in the [[2002]] novel ''[[The Approaching Storm]]'', written by [[Alan Dean Foster]]. He would later receive an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]], and released on [[December]] 8, [[2008]].
